get.description:
Basic health check endpoint.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).

Returns:
    dict: Health status of the API service.

Example response:
    {"status": "healthy", "service": "api"}

Database connectivity health check with 10s result caching.

Caches successful results for 10 seconds to prevent connection pool
exhaustion from frequent health check polling.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    db: Database session dependency.

Returns:
    dict: Health status of the database connection.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 503 if database is not accessible.

schema.title: Response Database Health Check Health Db Get

get.description:
Redis health check with 10s result caching.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).

Returns:
    dict: Health status of the Redis connection.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 503 if Redis is not accessible.

schema.title: Response Health Redis Health Redis Get

get.description:
Readiness check for all application dependencies.

Verifies that the application is ready to serve requests by checking
all critical dependencies (database, configuration, Redis, etc.).

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    db: Database session dependency.

Returns:
    dict: Readiness status with environment and dependency information.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 503 if any dependency is not ready.

schema.title: Response Readiness Check Health Ready Get

post.description: Authenticate user with email and password. Returns JWT tokens.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token.
Revoke the current access token, effectively logging out.
Reset a user's password (admin only).
Seed demo users (development only, admin-only, no-op if users exist).

schema.title: Response Seed Demo Users Api V1 Auth Seed Post

get.description: List all users with pagination and filters (admin only).

post.description: Create a new user (admin only).

get.description: Get a single user by ID (admin only).

delete.description:
Delete user data for GDPR right-to-erasure compliance.

Admin-only. Permanently removes user record and clears associated
Redis tracking data. Cannot delete own account.

post.description:
Create a chat completion.

Accepts a list of messages and returns the agent's response
in OpenAI-compatible format.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    body: Chat completion request with messages.
    service: Agent service instance (injected).

Returns:
    Chat completion response with the agent's message.

get.description:
List available models.

Returns the currently configured LLM model information.

Returns:
    Dictionary with model list in OpenAI-compatible format.

schema.title: Response List Models V1 Models Get

get.description:
Get real-time vehicle positions, optionally filtered by feed and/or route.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    route_id: Optional GTFS route ID to filter results.
    feed_id: Optional feed source to filter results (e.g., "riga").

Returns:
    VehiclePositionsResponse with vehicle positions.

Raises:
    TransitDataError: Mapped to HTTP 503 by global exception handler.
List configured transit feeds and their status.

Get historical positions for a specific vehicle within a time range.

Requires admin, dispatcher, or editor role. Rate limited to 10/minute.

Args:
    request: HTTP request (rate limiting).
    vehicle_id: Fleet vehicle identifier.
    from_time: ISO 8601 start time (UTC).
    to_time: ISO 8601 end time (UTC).
    limit: Maximum positions to return (1-10000, default 1000).
    db: Async database session.

Returns:
    VehicleHistoryResponse with ordered position history.

Get aggregated delay trend for a route over a time range.

Uses TimescaleDB time_bucket for efficient time-series aggregation.
Rate limited to 10/minute.

Args:
    request: HTTP request (rate limiting).
    route_id: GTFS route identifier.
    from_time: ISO 8601 start time (UTC).
    to_time: ISO 8601 end time (UTC).
    interval_minutes: Time bucket size (5-1440 minutes, default 60).
    db: Async database session.

Returns:
    RouteDelayTrendResponse with time-bucketed delay data.

get.description: Return all stops in a single response (for map display).

List operational events with optional date range and driver filters.

Requires admin or dispatcher role. Operational data is restricted.

Export schedule data as NeTEx EPIP XML.
Get real-time vehicle positions as SIRI-VM XML.
Get stop departure predictions as SIRI-SM XML.
Get export availability status with entity counts.

Get fleet status breakdown with operational alerts.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    db: Async database session.

Returns:
    FleetSummaryResponse with counts by type/status and alerts.

Get driver coverage breakdown with expiry alerts.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    db: Async database session.

Returns:
    DriverSummaryResponse with counts by shift/status and alerts.

Get on-time adherence metrics from live GTFS-RT data.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    route_id: Optional GTFS route ID for single-route analysis.
    date: Service date as YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to today (Riga timezone).
    time_from: Start of analysis window (HH:MM).
    time_until: End of analysis window (HH:MM).
    db: Async database session.

Returns:
    OnTimePerformanceResponse with per-route and network metrics.

Get combined dashboard summary with fleet, driver, and on-time data.

On-time performance degrades gracefully — if transit data is unavailable,
the overview still returns fleet and driver summaries with an empty on-time
section.

Args:
    request: The incoming HTTP request (used for rate limiting).
    db: Async database session.

Returns:
    AnalyticsOverviewResponse with all three summaries.

Root endpoint providing API information.

Returns:
    Dict containing application name, version, and docs URL.

AnalyticsOverviewResponse.title: AnalyticsOverviewResponse
AnalyticsOverviewResponse.description: Combined dashboard summary with fleet, driver, and on-time data.

CalendarDateCreate.description: Schema for creating a calendar date exception.

CalendarDateResponse.description: Schema for calendar date exception responses.

ChatCompletionChoice.title: ChatCompletionChoice
ChatCompletionChoice.description:
A single completion choice in the response.

Attributes:
    index: The index of this choice in the list.
    message: The assistant's response message.
    finish_reason: Why the completion stopped.

ChatCompletionRequest.title: ChatCompletionRequest
ChatCompletionRequest.description:
Request body for the chat completions endpoint.

Attributes:
    messages: List of messages in the conversation (at least one required).
    model: Optional model override (ignored in MVP, uses server config).

ChatCompletionResponse.title: ChatCompletionResponse
ChatCompletionResponse.description:
Response from the chat completions endpoint.

Follows the OpenAI Chat Completions API response format.

Attributes:
    id: Unique identifier for this completion.
    object: Object type (always "chat.completion").
    created: Unix timestamp when the completion was created.
    model: The model used for the completion.
    choices: List of completion choices.
    usage: Token usage information.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description:
A single message in a chat conversation.

Attributes:
    role: The role of the message sender.
    content: The text content of the message.

ExportMetadata.title: ExportMetadata
ExportMetadata.description:
Metadata returned by the compliance status endpoint.

Attributes:
    format: Export format identifier.
    version: Standard version (e.g. "1.2" for NeTEx EPIP, "2.0" for SIRI).
    codespace: NeTEx codespace prefix for element IDs.
    generated_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of when metadata was generated.
    entity_counts: Count of entities available for export by type.

GTFSImportResponse.title: GTFSImportResponse
GTFSImportResponse.description:
Response schema for GTFS import operation.

Reports total counts and created/updated breakdown for each entity type.
Merge strategy: existing entities matched by GTFS ID are updated in place,
new entities are created, entities not in the ZIP are preserved.

LoginResponse.title: LoginResponse
LoginResponse.description:
Successful login response — returned to frontend auth.ts.

NOTE: Tokens are returned in the response body (not httpOnly cookies) because
Auth.js JWT strategy encrypts and stores them in its own httpOnly session cookie.
The refresh_token is never exposed to client-side JavaScript directly.

TransitFeedStatus.title: TransitFeedStatus
TransitFeedStatus.description:
Status of a single configured transit feed.

Attributes:
    feed_id: Unique identifier for the feed (e.g., "riga").
    operator_name: Human-readable operator name.
    enabled: Whether the feed is actively polled.
    poll_interval_seconds: Polling frequency in seconds.

TransitFeedsResponse.description:
Response for transit feeds status endpoint.

Attributes:

UsageInfo.description:

Attributes:

    completion_tokens: Tokens generated in the response.
    total_tokens: Total tokens used.

fleet_number.title: Fleet Number
fleet_number.description: Unique fleet identifier matching GTFS-RT vehicle_id

vehicle_type.title: Vehicle Type
vehicle_type.description: Vehicle type: bus, trolleybus, or tram

qualified_route_ids.title: Qualified Route Ids
qualified_route_ids.description: Comma-separated GTFS route IDs this vehicle is qualified for

VehicleHistoryResponse.title: VehicleHistoryResponse
VehicleHistoryResponse.description: Response for vehicle position history query.

VehiclePosition.title: VehiclePosition
VehiclePosition.description:
A single vehicle's real-time position for map rendering.

Attributes:
    vehicle_id: Fleet vehicle identifier (e.g., "4521").
    route_id: GTFS route identifier.
    route_short_name: Human-readable route number (e.g., "22").
    route_type: GTFS route type (0=tram, 3=bus, 11=trolleybus).
    latitude: WGS84 latitude.
    longitude: WGS84 longitude.
    bearing: Compass heading in degrees (0-360), if available.
    speed_kmh: Speed in km/h, if available.
    delay_seconds: Schedule deviation (positive=late, negative=early).
    current_status: One of "IN_TRANSIT_TO", "STOPPED_AT", "INCOMING_AT".
    next_stop_name: Name of next stop, if resolvable.
    current_stop_name: Name of current/nearest stop, if resolvable.
    timestamp: ISO 8601 when position was measured.

VehiclePositionsResponse.title: VehiclePositionsResponse
VehiclePositionsResponse.description:
Response wrapper for vehicle positions endpoint.

Attributes:
    count: Number of vehicles in response.
    vehicles: List of vehicle positions.
    fetched_at: ISO 8601 server time when data was assembled.
    feed_id: Feed filter applied, if any.
